transcript.clean_text: stage two of the 90th anniversary edition of Perkins would see the Riders leave basavil southeast of Paris and head towards Fontainebleau first time that the race would finish there since the 1960s 163.7 kilometers with the parkour perfect if the wind was blowing strongly a fairly flat day at a late intermediate Sprint with six four and two bonus seconds available before the 10 6 and 4 on the line and more importantly the stage win the wind wasn't howling but it was blowing in the right direction and even just a little bit of a breeze was enough to allow Borah hand screwer to split things briefly with 70 and a half kilometers to go things would come back together though and alone man up the road Jonas grigor would be allowed to take the two King of the mountains prizes and put himself into the polka dot Jersey behind the atmosphere the mood was nervous all day long certainly after the first split had occurred and that meant that the Breakaway the lone man will be brought back with 54 kilometers still to go his job done in terms of a Podium appearance and that polka dot Jersey but behind the nerves would continue and they would Fray because pierlator would go down not in the Crosswinds but in a nervy moment through a town the Frenchman would be helped by a few teammates bringing him back and then the wind speed would eventually start to increase that wouldn't last long though and all the real action came as Tim Wellens came to the front passed on a message and UAE Emirates would be going for more bonus seconds are going this time on a flat intermediate Sprint and proving that he can do just about everything brilliantly finishing in front of Michael Matthews and NATA for hoydonk he takes six more bonus seconds and very briefly move himself into the virtual overall lead the 12 and a half kilometers that remained past by very quickly indeed indeed a crazy downhill run in with speeds of around 90 kilometers per hour followed by a couple of traffic Islands inside the last kilometer and a half they caused a crash with all sorts of nerves and moves left and right the ride is down thankfully able to be getting back up but seeing Riders called out inside the final 500 meters around about Sam Bennett losing the wheel of Danny from Popple Tim melir already caught up by this point as well quite a few big name sprinters not able to have a go Mass Peterson was though for the second day in succession the former world champion was right up there he came through the middle and just ahead of Olaf koi managed to win stage two of bakines the sage win will also be enough to see him take the lead the first Danish Rider to lead this race for 53 years older hitter the only other Danish leader of paranis like in 1970. good placings for the likes of Magnus Court Leonel tamino Michael Matthews and company as well Dan mcleay with a good Sprint for arcare samsik through the center medicine koi Court McClay Tammy new in the top five Matthews vonaberg and dimar making up the top 10 on the day yellow Jersey on the back of mass Peterson in charge and they'll be last to start as we move to the new team time trial format on day three the GC with a two second Gap and the team time trial starting and finishing in donpia on Broly 32.2 kilometers the distance one time check on the way but the novelty in that the first ride at across the line sees his time taken and given in the general classification so will it cause any changes will we see any new Tactical approaches wait to find out and if you'd like to watch the race you'll be able to do so on gcn Plus
